chunks: ================================================================================ CREATORA — COMPLETE PRODUCT & FEATURE DOCUMENTATION ================================================================================ Purpose of this document: This document is written to be used as a knowledge base for training/grounding a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) chatbot that answers user questions about the Creatora website/product. It describes every feature, screen, API endpoint, and workflow found in the actual source code of the project (both backend and frontend repositories) as of the analysis date. Where the marketing/landing page describes something that is NOT actually implemented in the working code (e.g., mock/demo data), this is explicitly flagged as "NOT LIVE / MOCK DATA" so the chatbot never tells a user that a fake feature is real. Sources analyzed: - Backend repository: https://github.com/prn9v/Creatora-Backend (NestJS + Prisma + PostgreSQL) - Frontend repository: https://github.com/prn9v/Creatora-Frontend (Next.js 16 / React 19) - Live backend: https://creatora-backend.onrender.com (Swagger docs at /docs) - Live frontend: https://creatora-frontend.vercel.app/ ================================================================================ 1. WHAT IS CREATORA? ================================================================================ Creatora is an AI-powered content creation platform built for individual content creators, influencers, and small brands. Its core promise is: "Learn your unique writing voice from your past content, then generate new, on-brand social media posts, video scripts, content ideas, and a weekly posting calendar that all sound authentically like you — not like generic AI output." The product is split into two parts: 1. A NestJS REST API backend that handles authentication, data

media posts, video scripts, content ideas, and a weekly posting calendar that all sound authentically like you — not like generic AI output." The product is split into two parts: 1. A NestJS REST API backend that handles authentication, data storage, web-scraping of past social posts, AI analysis (via Google Gemini), and AI content generation (via Gemini and an external FastAPI microservice). 2. A Next.js frontend (React 19, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui-style components) that provides the onboarding flow and the day-to-day creator dashboard. ================================================================================ 2. HIGH-LEVEL USER JOURNEY ================================================================================ 1. Visitor lands on the marketing homepage (Hero, Features, How-It-Works, Pricing, CTA sections). 2. User signs up (email + password) or logs in. 3. First-time users are guided through a two-step ONBOARDING flow: Step 1 — Brand Setup: choose brand tone, niche, and target audience. Step 2 — Past Post Upload: paste URLs of at least 3 real past posts from different platforms (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram, Blog, Newsletter, X Thread). Each URL is scraped and analyzed individually. Once at least 3 posts are successfully analyzed, the user clicks "Analyze & Train AI", which triggers a writing-style analysis that builds their personal "Brand Profile" (their AI voice model). 4. After onboarding, the user lands on the main Dashboard, and from there can use the following authenticated tools via the left-hand sidebar: - Dashboard

which triggers a writing-style analysis that builds their personal "Brand Profile" (their AI voice model). 4. After onboarding, the user lands on the main Dashboard, and from there can use the following authenticated tools via the left-hand sidebar: - Dashboard (overview/stats) - Content Ideas (AI + trending idea generation) - Generate Post (single Instagram-style post + video script + schedule) - Generated Posts (history/library of everything generated) - Weekly Planner (drag-and-drop style weekly content calendar) - Profile (personal settings) - Account (billing/plan — currently mock data, see section 8) ================================================================================ 3. AUTHENTICATION & ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT ================================================================================ Backend module: `src/auth` (NestJS controller: `AuthController`, route prefix: `/v1/users/auth`) Features: - Sign Up: email, password (min 6 characters), optional display name. Passwords are hashed using Node's built-in `scrypt` algorithm with a random salt (format: `hash.salt`), NOT bcrypt. - Log In: email + password. On success, a JWT access token is issued and set as an HTTP-only, secure, SameSite=None cookie named `access_token` (1 hour expiry). The response body also returns basic user info (id, email, plan, name) and `success: true`. - Get Current User ("Me"): `GET /v1/users/auth/me` — requires a valid JWT cookie. Returns full user profile including brand profile, past posts, generated posts, content ideas, and weekly plans. - Refresh Token: `POST /v1/users/auth/refresh-token` — issues a new JWT cookie for the currently authenticated user.

`GET /v1/users/auth/me` — requires a valid JWT cookie. Returns full user profile including brand profile, past posts, generated posts, content ideas, and weekly plans. - Refresh Token: `POST /v1/users/auth/refresh-token` — issues a new JWT cookie for the currently authenticated user. - Update Password: `PUT /v1/users/auth/update-password` — requires current password, new password, and confirmation. Rejects if the new password is the same as the old one, or if confirmation doesn't match. - Forgot Password: `POST /v1/users/auth/forgot-password` — generates a 6-digit OTP (numeric), valid for 15 minutes, and emails it to the user via the Brevo transactional email API ("Creatora Password Reset Verification Code" email template). - Reset Password: `POST /v1/users/auth/reset-password` — accepts email, OTP, and new password; validates the OTP and expiry before updating the password. - Log Out: `POST /v1/users/auth/logout` — clears the `access_token` cookie. - Delete Account: `DELETE /v1/users/auth` — requires the user's current password for confirmation. On success, sends an "Account Deletion Confirmation" email and permanently deletes the user record (and, via Prisma relations, associated data) from the database. This action is irreversible. Security notes for the chatbot to know: - JWT tokens are stored in an HTTP-only cookie (not accessible to JavaScript), which mitigates XSS token theft. - Session/token lifetime is 1 hour before a refresh is needed. - There is currently no visible email verification step

to know: - JWT tokens are stored in an HTTP-only cookie (not accessible to JavaScript), which mitigates XSS token theft. - Session/token lifetime is 1 hour before a refresh is needed. - There is currently no visible email verification step on signup (no "verify your email" flow was found in the codebase) — signup is immediate. - There is no third-party/social login (Google/GitHub OAuth) implemented; only email + password authentication exists. ================================================================================ 4. USER PROFILE MANAGEMENT ================================================================================ Backend module: `src/profile` (route prefix: `/v1/users/profile`) - Update Profile: `PUT /v1/users/profile/update-profile` — lets the user update their display Name, Email, Bio (max 500 characters), and Profile Image URL (typically populated after uploading through the Image Upload endpoint described below). - Note: In the frontend Profile page, the Email field is shown but disabled for direct editing, with the hint "Contact support to change your email" — even though the backend DTO technically allows an email field to be passed. Frontend Profile screen (`/profile`) features: - Avatar upload (click-to-upload profile picture): validates that the file is an image and is under 2MB, uploads it to Cloudinary via the Image Upload endpoint, then saves the resulting URL to the user's profile. - Editable fields: Display Name, Bio (email is read-only in the UI). - "Save Changes" button to persist profile edits. - Change Password section:

to Cloudinary via the Image Upload endpoint, then saves the resulting URL to the user's profile. - Editable fields: Display Name, Bio (email is read-only in the UI). - "Save Changes" button to persist profile edits. - Change Password section: current password, new password, confirm new password, each with a show/hide (eye icon) toggle. - "Danger Zone": permanently delete the account, requiring the user to re-enter their password before the delete button becomes enabled. - Displays "Joined <Month Year>" based on account creation date. ================================================================================ 5. IMAGE UPLOAD (Cloudinary) ================================================================================ Backend module: `src/common/image` (route: `POST /image/upload`) - Accepts a single multipart/form-data file field named `image`. - Validated file types: jpg, jpeg, png, webp, gif. - Maximum file size enforced by the API layer: 10MB (NestJS validator), but the underlying Cloudinary upload service enforces a stricter 2MB limit and will reject anything larger with "File too large. Maximum allowed size is 2MB." - On success, returns `{ url, publicId }` — a secure Cloudinary CDN URL and the Cloudinary public ID (useful for later deletion). - Images are automatically optimized on upload (`quality: auto`, `fetch_format: auto`) to reduce file size while preserving visual quality. - Used today specifically for profile picture uploads. ================================================================================ 6. ONBOARDING FLOW (BRAND VOICE SETUP) ================================================================================ This is arguably Creatora's signature feature: teaching the AI to write

upload (`quality: auto`, `fetch_format: auto`) to reduce file size while preserving visual quality. - Used today specifically for profile picture uploads. ================================================================================ 6. ONBOARDING FLOW (BRAND VOICE SETUP) ================================================================================ This is arguably Creatora's signature feature: teaching the AI to write like the specific creator, rather than generating generic AI content. -------------------------------------------------------------------------- 6.1 Step One — Brand Setup (`/onboarding/brand` page, brand tone/niche/audience) -------------------------------------------------------------------------- Backend endpoint: `POST /onboarding/brand` Frontend page: two-step progress indicator, Step 1 of 2. The user selects/enters: - Brand Tone (single choice, one of four options with descriptions shown to the user): • Professional — "Clear, authoritative, and business-focused" • Casual — "Friendly, conversational, and relatable" • Inspiring — "Motivational, uplifting, and energizing" • Educational — "Informative, clear, and structured" - Niche (free text, e.g., "Tech startups") - Target Audience (free text, longer description, e.g., "Early-stage founders and product managers") This data is saved to (or updates) the user's `BrandProfile` database record. All three fields are optional at the database level but required by the frontend form before the user can continue. -------------------------------------------------------------------------- 6.2 Step Two — Upload Past Content Samples (`/onboarding/posts` page) -------------------------------------------------------------------------- Backend endpoints: - `POST /onboarding/add-post` — extract & save a single past post from a URL - `POST /onboarding/analyze-profile` — analyze all saved posts and build/ update the AI writing-style ("Brand Profile") model How it

Content Samples (`/onboarding/posts` page) -------------------------------------------------------------------------- Backend endpoints: - `POST /onboarding/add-post` — extract & save a single past post from a URL - `POST /onboarding/analyze-profile` — analyze all saved posts and build/ update the AI writing-style ("Brand Profile") model How it works: 1. The user is shown 3 pre-populated post "slots" (defaulting to Twitter, LinkedIn, and Instagram) and can add more via "Add Another Sample Post". 2. For each slot, the user picks a source platform from: Twitter/X, X Thread, LinkedIn, Instagram, Blog/Article, or Newsletter (email), then pastes the URL of one of their own real past posts. 3. Clicking "Analyze Post" sends the URL to the backend, which: a. Detects the platform automatically from the URL pattern (also supports Facebook and YouTube URLs even though they are not directly selectable in the current UI dropdown). b. Scrapes the content using the Apify web-scraping platform — separate Apify Actors are used per platform: - Instagram → `apify/instagram-scraper` (caption, media URL, likes, comments, timestamp, author username) - Twitter/X → `apify/twitter-scraper` (falls back to `quacker/twitter-scraper` if the primary actor fails) — captures tweet text, author, likes, retweets, replies, timestamp - LinkedIn, YouTube, Facebook, and generic blogs/websites each have their own dedicated scraping logic (with LinkedIn/Facebook text cleanup for truncated "…see more" text, and generic HTML/text cleanup for blogs) - A generic fallback scraper

text, author, likes, retweets, replies, timestamp - LinkedIn, YouTube, Facebook, and generic blogs/websites each have their own dedicated scraping logic (with LinkedIn/Facebook text cleanup for truncated "…see more" text, and generic HTML/text cleanup for blogs) - A generic fallback scraper is used if the primary platform-specific scraper fails, so the flow is resilient to scraping errors. c. Runs a deep AI content analysis on the scraped text using Google's Gemini model (`gemini-2.5-flash`). This analysis produces a detailed structured JSON profile of that single post, including: tone (from a rich vocabulary like Professional, Witty, Satirical, Melancholic, Luxurious, Educational, Urgent, Minimalist, Aspirational, Aggressive, Whimsical), sentiment, 2-4 key abstract themes, writing style (Narrative, Direct, Conversational, Academic, Poetic, Technical), target audience description, call-to-action, hashtags found, mentions found, word count, readability score, primary emotional appeal (FOMO, Nostalgia, Validation, Curiosity, Outrage, Hope, Exclusivity), and a short structural breakdown (e.g. "Hook -> Story -> CTA"). d. Saves the raw content, metadata, and this analysis to the database as a "Past Post" record tied to the user. 4. Once at least 3 posts show "Analyzed Successfully", the "Analyze & Train AI" button becomes enabled. Clicking it triggers `analyze-profile`, which: a. Retrieves ALL of the user's saved past posts (must be at least 3, or the backend returns an error). b. Sends all post contents together to Gemini with

& Train AI" button becomes enabled. Clicking it triggers `analyze-profile`, which: a. Retrieves ALL of the user's saved past posts (must be at least 3, or the backend returns an error). b. Sends all post contents together to Gemini with a prompt asking it to extract a single, consistent overall "writing voice" fingerprint: style summary, tone (Professional/Casual/Inspiring/Educational), storytelling style (direct/narrative/conversational), humor usage (boolean), vocabulary complexity (simple/moderate/advanced), formality score (0 to 1), emotional tone, common phrases the creator tends to use, topic preferences, and average sentence length. c. This becomes (or updates) the user's persistent "Brand Profile" — the foundation every future piece of generated content is built on. 5. While training runs, the UI shows a dedicated animated loading screen ("Training Your AI Model... analyzing writing patterns, sentence structures, and tone from N distinct samples... Finalizing semantic analysis...") before redirecting to the Dashboard. 6. A "Skip setup (Not recommended)" link exists, letting a user bypass onboarding and go straight to the dashboard — but AI content generation features that depend on a Brand Profile will not work until the profile is completed (they return an explicit error telling the user to finish onboarding first). Note for chatbot: the platform explicitly states past-post data is "encrypted and used solely for training your personal model" (per the UI copy) — the chatbot

is completed (they return an explicit error telling the user to finish onboarding first). Note for chatbot: the platform explicitly states past-post data is "encrypted and used solely for training your personal model" (per the UI copy) — the chatbot should present this framing if a user asks about data privacy during onboarding, without over-claiming security details that aren't verifiable from the code alone. ================================================================================ 7. BRAND PROFILE (THE AI "VOICE MODEL") ================================================================================ This is a database record (`BrandProfile`) unique per user, containing: - niche, audience, tone (enum: PROFESSIONAL / CASUAL / INSPIRING / EDUCATIONAL) - AI-generated styleSummary (plain-language description of their voice) - avgSentenceLength, vocabularyComplexity (simple/moderate/advanced) - commonPhrases (list of phrases they tend to repeat) - emotionalTone, formalityScore (0–1 scale) - humorUsage (boolean) - storytellingStyle (direct/narrative/conversational) - topicPreferences (list of topics they gravitate to) Every downstream AI generation feature (Content Ideas, Post Generation, Weekly Planner) reads from this Brand Profile so the output matches the creator's real voice rather than generic AI-sounding text. If a user has not completed onboarding (no Brand Profile exists), attempts to use Ideas generation, Post generation, or Weekly Planning will fail with a clear error asking them to complete their brand profile first. ================================================================================ 8. DASHBOARD (HOME SCREEN) ================================================================================ Frontend route: `/dashboard` Backend data source: `GET /v1/users/auth/me` What it shows: - Personalized greeting/header using

Post generation, or Weekly Planning will fail with a clear error asking them to complete their brand profile first. ================================================================================ 8. DASHBOARD (HOME SCREEN) ================================================================================ Frontend route: `/dashboard` Backend data source: `GET /v1/users/auth/me` What it shows: - Personalized greeting/header using the user's name. - Four key stat cards: 1. "Total Posts Generated" — lifetime count of AI-generated posts. 2. "Remaining Credits" — (creditsLimit − creditsUsed), with the total limit shown as a subtext (e.g., "of 50"). 3. "Current Plan" — the user's subscription plan (FREE / CREATOR / PRO). 4. "Posts Generated This Month" — count of posts created in the current calendar month. - Brand Profile summary card (niche, tone, audience, style highlights). - Weekly Planner preview widget (a lightweight glance at the current week's planned posts / posting cadence). - Quick Actions panel (shortcuts to Generate Post, Content Ideas, Weekly Planner, etc.). - Recent Posts list — the 3 most recently generated posts, each showing platform, a short content preview, and relative time (e.g., "2 hours ago"). Clicking a recent post opens a Post Detail modal with the full content. Credits system: Every user starts on the FREE plan with a default limit of 50 generation credits (`creditsLimit`, `creditsUsed` fields on the User model). Each successful "Generate Post" call consumes 1 credit. If `creditsUsed >= creditsLimit`, further generation

full content. Credits system: Every user starts on the FREE plan with a default limit of 50 generation credits (`creditsLimit`, `creditsUsed` fields on the User model). Each successful "Generate Post" call consumes 1 credit. If `creditsUsed >= creditsLimit`, further generation requests are blocked with a "Credit limit reached" error until the limit is reset/increased (e.g. by upgrading plans). ================================================================================ 9. CONTENT IDEAS (`/ideas`) ================================================================================ Backend module: `src/ideas-generation` (route prefix: `/ideas`) This screen has three sections: 9.1 AI Generated Ideas - Button: "Generate New AI Ideas" → `POST /ideas/generate` - Uses the user's Brand Profile (niche, audience, tone, storytelling style) and asks Gemini (`gemini-2.5-flash`) to produce exactly 15 highly specific content ideas in one batch, following a deliberate content-mix formula: • 4 Reels / TikTok-style short-form video ideas (under 60 seconds) • 3 Carousel post ideas (multi-slide educational/storytelling) • 2 Talking-head / POV video ideas • 2 Behind-the-scenes / Day-in-the-life ideas • 2 "Trend hijack" ideas (adapting a current trend/audio/challenge to the creator's niche) • 2 Story / Poll / interactive post ideas - Each idea includes: a title, a scroll-stopping hook (first 2 seconds), a 60-100 word description of the concept/story/why it will resonate/ execution tips, the format, the unique narrative "angle", and a suggested call-to-action. - All generated ideas are saved to the database tagged `source: "AI"`. 9.2 Trending

hook (first 2 seconds), a 60-100 word description of the concept/story/why it will resonate/ execution tips, the format, the unique narrative "angle", and a suggested call-to-action. - All generated ideas are saved to the database tagged `source: "AI"`. 9.2 Trending Ideas - Button: "Generate Trending Ideas" → `GET /ideas/trending` - Calls an external, separately-hosted generative model service (Creatora's own "idea generation" microservice) that factors in the user's full Brand Profile (niche, audience, style summary, average sentence length, common phrases, emotional tone, formality score, humor usage, storytelling style, topic preferences, vocabulary complexity, tone) and requests 10 trending ideas informed by current platform trends (the ContentIdea schema supports fields like `trendSource` — e.g. Reddit, YouTube, Instagram — and `trendTitle`, suggesting the underlying model is designed to surface ideas based on real trending topics/sounds on those platforms). - Saved to the database tagged `source: "TRENDING"`. 9.3 Your Favorites - Any idea (AI or Trending) can be "favorited" by clicking its heart icon, via `POST /ideas/favorite` (toggles favorite status). - `GET /ideas/favorites` returns all of a user's favorited ideas, newest first. This same favorites list feeds directly into the Weekly Planner (see Section 10) as a pool of ideas the user can schedule for specific days. - Clicking any idea card (in any of the three sections) opens a detail dialog showing the full

list feeds directly into the Weekly Planner (see Section 10) as a pool of ideas the user can schedule for specific days. - Clicking any idea card (in any of the three sections) opens a detail dialog showing the full idea (hook, description, format, angle, CTA, platform, trend source if applicable) with a favorite toggle inside the dialog too. ================================================================================ 10. GENERATE POST (`/generate`) — SINGLE-POST AI GENERATION ================================================================================ Backend module: `src/content-generation` (route prefix: `/content-generation`, JWT-protected) This is the flagship "one-click content generation" feature, currently built around Instagram as the primary output format. 10.1 Generating content — `POST /content-generation/generate` - Requires the user to already have a completed Brand Profile and at least 1 remaining credit. - The backend gathers the user's Brand Profile + all their Past Posts, and sends this context to an external FastAPI microservice ("creatora-fast-api-post-generation", a separate deployed service) at its `/generate-post` endpoint. - That service returns three parallel content variants for the SAME underlying idea, all matched to the creator's brand voice: 1. TEXT variant — a caption + hashtags (platform-agnostic short text post). 2. IMAGE variant — a caption, hashtags, an AI-generated image prompt, and a generated image URL (ready to be used as an Instagram feed post). 3. VIDEO variant — a hook line, caption, full voiceover script, general shooting instructions, an audience-engagement

IMAGE variant — a caption, hashtags, an AI-generated image prompt, and a generated image URL (ready to be used as an Instagram feed post). 3. VIDEO variant — a hook line, caption, full voiceover script, general shooting instructions, an audience-engagement question, and hashtags (this becomes the source data for the Video Script feature below). - The result is saved to the database as a `GeneratedPost` record (the raw JSON of all three variants is stored as the post's content), 1 credit is deducted from the user, and the frontend receives a formatted "Instagram Preview" object simulating how the post would look in an Instagram feed (username, avatar, post image, caption, hashtags, and — importantly — mock/randomized like & comment counts purely for visual preview purposes, NOT real Instagram engagement data). 10.2 On the Generate screen, once content is generated, the user can: - View an Instagram-style live preview card of the generated image post. - "Download Image" — downloads the generated image file directly to their device. - "Copy Caption" — copies the caption + hashtags to the clipboard as one block of text. - "Post to Instagram" (helper flow) — this does NOT auto-post via any Instagram API integration (Creatora has no official Instagram Graph API connection for automatic publishing). Instead, it is a convenience flow that: (1) auto-downloads

one block of text. - "Post to Instagram" (helper flow) — this does NOT auto-post via any Instagram API integration (Creatora has no official Instagram Graph API connection for automatic publishing). Instead, it is a convenience flow that: (1) auto-downloads the generated image, (2) copies the caption to the clipboard, and (3) opens Instagram (either the native app via a deep link on mobile, or Instagram's web "create post" page on desktop) so the user can manually select the downloaded image and paste the caption themselves. - "View Video Script" — fetches `GET /content-generation/:postId/video- script`, which breaks the raw video hook/script/CTA into a structured, numbered shooting script (see 10.3). - "View Posting Schedule" — fetches `GET /content-generation/:postId/ posting-schedule`, an AI-generated recommendation on exactly when to post this content (see 10.4). - "Regenerate" — runs generation again for a brand-new variant (also consumes another credit). 10.3 Video Script breakdown The raw AI video script is automatically parsed into a scene-by-scene shooting guide: - Scene 1 is always the "Opening Hook" (~3 seconds, close-up shot type, with concrete shooting tips like "use good lighting, ensure audio is clear, start with energy"). - The middle of the script is automatically split into up to 3 "Main Content" scenes (~8 seconds each), cycling through shot types (medium, b-roll, wide) and providing scene-specific visual notes

good lighting, ensure audio is clear, start with energy"). - The middle of the script is automatically split into up to 3 "Main Content" scenes (~8 seconds each), cycling through shot types (medium, b-roll, wide) and providing scene-specific visual notes (e.g., "b-roll footage of product in use", "wide shot showing context/environment"). - The final scene is always a "Call to Action" scene (~4 seconds, talking-head shot type) using the audience-engagement question as the voiceover, with shooting tips like "be energetic, make eye contact with camera, smile". - Total video duration is automatically summed from all scene durations and displayed (e.g., "0:25"). - The response also separately includes the full caption and hashtag set intended for the video post. 10.4 AI Posting Schedule Uses Gemini (`gemini-2.0-flash-exp`) with the user's Brand Profile (niche, audience, tone) and today's date to recommend: - The best specific date/day-of-week/time-window to post the IMAGE variant, with a stated reason (e.g., "Evening hours see 40% higher engagement for food/lifestyle content"). - The best specific date/day/time-window to post the VIDEO variant. - The recommended GAP between the two posts (in days/hours) with reasoning (to avoid over-posting/spamming followers). - A suggestion for the user's NEXT piece of content after these two (what content type, when, and why). - A general "Best Posting Times" table broken down by day of week,

(in days/hours) with reasoning (to avoid over-posting/spamming followers). - A suggestion for the user's NEXT piece of content after these two (what content type, when, and why). - A general "Best Posting Times" table broken down by day of week, each with recommended time slots and an engagement-level label (High / Very High / Medium). - If the Gemini call fails or returns invalid data, the backend falls back to a sensible hardcoded default schedule (tomorrow for the image post, a couple of days later for video, evening/lunch time slots) so the feature never simply breaks for the user. ================================================================================ 11. GENERATED POSTS LIBRARY (`/generated-posts`) ================================================================================ Backend endpoints (route prefix: `/v1/users/generated-posts`): - `GET /v1/users/generated-posts` — paginated list of every post the user has ever generated. Supports: • `search` — free-text search • `sort` — `asc` or `desc` • `orderBy` — field to sort by (e.g. `createdAt`, `platform`) • `page` / `limit` — standard pagination (frontend defaults to 9 posts per page) - `GET /v1/users/generated-posts/:id` — fetch one specific generated post by its ID. Frontend features on this screen: - A search box (debounced ~400ms before triggering a new search) to filter posts by content. - Prev/Next pagination controls with a "Page X of Y (Z total)" indicator. - A responsive grid of Post Cards, each rendering the generated content (parsed

search box (debounced ~400ms before triggering a new search) to filter posts by content. - Prev/Next pagination controls with a "Page X of Y (Z total)" indicator. - A responsive grid of Post Cards, each rendering the generated content (parsed from the stored JSON) in a readable preview format. - Empty state: if the user has never generated anything, shows a friendly "No Posts Yet" message with a direct "Generate Your First Post" button linking to `/generate`. ================================================================================ 12. WEEKLY PLANNER (`/planner`) ================================================================================ Backend module: `src/weekly-planner` (route prefix: `/planner`, all routes JWT-protected) This is Creatora's content-calendar / scheduling-assistant feature. It is NOT a social-media auto-publishing tool (no posts are actually pushed to any platform automatically) — it is an AI-assisted planning board that helps the creator decide what to post, when, across the week, and track whether they actually posted it. 12.1 Generating a weekly plan - `POST /planner/generate` — body can optionally include a `weekStart` date (YYYY-MM-DD); if omitted, it defaults to the Monday of the current week. - The backend gathers, in parallel: the user's Brand Profile, all of the user's FAVORITED content ideas (from the Ideas screen), and their 10 most recent generated posts. - It builds a detailed prompt (via a dedicated prompt-builder, `plan-generator.prompt.ts`) and calls Gemini (`gemini-2.5-flash`, JSON response mode, temperature 0.7) to produce a

of the user's FAVORITED content ideas (from the Ideas screen), and their 10 most recent generated posts. - It builds a detailed prompt (via a dedicated prompt-builder, `plan-generator.prompt.ts`) and calls Gemini (`gemini-2.5-flash`, JSON response mode, temperature 0.7) to produce a full 7-day content plan. - The plan (Prisma model `WeeklyPlan`) is uniquely keyed per user per `weekStart` — regenerating for a week that already has a plan overwrites it ("regenerate the weekly plan"). - The AI-produced plan JSON includes: • A one-sentence overall `summary`/rationale for the week's strategy. • A list of `posts` (scheduled slots), each with: - day of week (0=Monday … 6=Sunday) - a specific scheduled time (24-hour "HH:MM") - target platform (Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, Blog, Email, or Thread) - content format (Reel, Carousel, Talking-head, BTS, Trend hijack, Story/Poll, POV, Behind-the-scenes, Day-in-the-life, or a custom string) - a title and caption - hashtags - a link back to the source ContentIdea (if the slot was built from one of the user's favorited ideas) or to an existing GeneratedPost (if reusing prior content) - a status: SCHEDULED, POSTED, or SKIPPED - an AI-written rationale explaining WHY this slot/time/format was chosen • A list of `reminders` — supporting tasks tied to specific days/times, each typed as HARVEST (e.g., "capture footage/content today"), ENGAGEMENT_CHECK (e.g., "reply to comments on yesterday's

POSTED, or SKIPPED - an AI-written rationale explaining WHY this slot/time/format was chosen • A list of `reminders` — supporting tasks tied to specific days/times, each typed as HARVEST (e.g., "capture footage/content today"), ENGAGEMENT_CHECK (e.g., "reply to comments on yesterday's post"), CONTENT_PREP, or GENERAL, with a title and description. • `unscheduledIdeaIds` — any of the user's favorited ideas that the AI deliberately did NOT place into this week's plan (kept available for manual scheduling — see the Unscheduled Ideas panel below). - Built-in platform/format "best posting window" heuristics (a hand-tuned lookup table in `schedule-optimizer.ts`) act as a soft constraint that guides (but doesn't strictly force) the AI's time choices — e.g., Instagram Reels default to Monday/Thursday mornings, LinkedIn posts default to Monday/Wednesday at 8:00 AM, Threads default to Monday/Thursday evenings, etc. The AI can override these defaults when it has good contextual reason to. - The backend defends against malformed AI output: it strips accidental markdown code fences, detects clearly truncated JSON and asks the user to regenerate rather than silently corrupting data, and guarantees every post/reminder gets a valid unique ID even if the AI omitted one. 12.2 Viewing and navigating plans - `GET /planner/:weekStart` — fetch the plan for a specific week (e.g. `2026-05-19`). Returns 404 if no plan has been generated for that week yet, in which

unique ID even if the AI omitted one. 12.2 Viewing and navigating plans - `GET /planner/:weekStart` — fetch the plan for a specific week (e.g. `2026-05-19`). Returns 404 if no plan has been generated for that week yet, in which case the frontend shows an "Empty Planner State" inviting the user to generate one. - `GET /planner/by-id/:planId` — fetch a plan directly by its database ID. - `GET /planner/history` — returns ALL of a user's previously generated weekly plans, newest first, shown in a "History" dialog so users can jump back to any past week's plan. - The frontend Planner screen has Prev Week / Next Week / "Today" (jump to current week) navigation controls, and displays the current week's date range as a label at the top. 12.3 Managing individual scheduled slots - `PATCH /planner/:planId/slots/:slotId` — edit any field of an existing slot (day, time, platform, title, caption, hashtags, status). - `PATCH /planner/:planId/slots/:slotId/posted` — one-click shortcut to mark a slot as POSTED. - `PATCH /planner/:planId/slots/:slotId/skipped` — one-click shortcut to mark a slot as SKIPPED (the user chose not to post it). - `POST /planner/:planId/slots` — manually add a brand-new post slot to an existing plan (not AI-generated — user-authored), optionally pre-filled from one of their favorited-but-unscheduled ideas. - `DELETE /planner/:planId/slots/:slotId` — remove a slot entirely. 12.4 Planner UI layout

post it). - `POST /planner/:planId/slots` — manually add a brand-new post slot to an existing plan (not AI-generated — user-authored), optionally pre-filled from one of their favorited-but-unscheduled ideas. - `DELETE /planner/:planId/slots/:slotId` — remove a slot entirely. 12.4 Planner UI layout - A 7-day week grid (Monday through Sunday) showing each day's scheduled post cards and reminder cards. - Each post card shows platform, format, time, title/caption snippet, and status, with quick actions to edit, delete, mark posted, or mark skipped. - A right-hand sidebar panel shows: • "Unscheduled Ideas" — favorited ideas the AI didn't place this week; clicking one opens the Add-Slot modal pre-filled with that idea's details so the user can manually schedule it into any day. • "Reminders" — the week's AI-generated reminder tasks. - "Add Slot" and "Edit Slot" are both presented as modal dialogs. - A loading skeleton is shown while a plan is being fetched, and a dedicated empty-state screen is shown when no plan exists yet for the selected week, with a prominent "Generate Plan" call-to-action. ================================================================================ 13. ACCOUNT & BILLING (`/account`) — CURRENTLY STATIC / MOCK DATA ================================================================================ IMPORTANT FOR THE CHATBOT: As implemented in the current codebase, the entire Account & Billing screen is a FRONT-END-ONLY MOCKUP. It is NOT wired up to any real payment processor (no Stripe/Paddle/Razorpay integration was

CURRENTLY STATIC / MOCK DATA ================================================================================ IMPORTANT FOR THE CHATBOT: As implemented in the current codebase, the entire Account & Billing screen is a FRONT-END-ONLY MOCKUP. It is NOT wired up to any real payment processor (no Stripe/Paddle/Razorpay integration was found in either repository) and does NOT reflect a user's real usage or plan in real time. Specifically, in the current source code: - The displayed "Current Plan" (shown as "Pro", $49/month), its feature list, "Next billing date", and the "Cancel Subscription" button are all hardcoded placeholder values in the frontend component — clicking these buttons does not call any backend endpoint. - The "Payment Method" card (showing a fake VISA card ending in 4242, expiring 12/26) is static placeholder UI. - The "Usage This Month" statistics (Posts Generated: 47, Ideas Generated: 23, Storage Used: 2.3GB of 10GB) are hardcoded sample numbers, not the authenticated user's real usage. - The "Billing History" table (Dec/Nov/Oct/Sep invoices) is a hardcoded sample list, not real transaction records. The chatbot should NOT tell users they can actually upgrade their plan, update payment methods, or download real invoices from this screen today — it should clarify this page currently shows example/demo data if a user asks about billing specifics, and should direct real plan/credit questions back to the Dashboard's "Remaining Credits" and "Current Plan" stat

download real invoices from this screen today — it should clarify this page currently shows example/demo data if a user asks about billing specifics, and should direct real plan/credit questions back to the Dashboard's "Remaining Credits" and "Current Plan" stat cards, which DO reflect real database values (`plan`, `creditsUsed`, `creditsLimit` on the User model). Real subscription plan tiers that DO exist at the data-model level: - FREE (default plan for all new sign-ups, 50 credits by default) - CREATOR - PRO (There is currently no visible backend endpoint to actually change a user's plan/tier — this appears to be a planned/future feature.) ================================================================================ 14. SIDEBAR NAVIGATION SUMMARY ================================================================================ Main navigation (top section): - Dashboard → /dashboard - Content Ideas → /ideas - Generate Post → /generate - Generated Posts → /generated-posts - Weekly Planner → /planner Bottom navigation: - Profile → /profile - Account → /account - Log out (clears the session cookie and redirects to the homepage) The sidebar can be collapsed/expanded (icon-only vs. icon+label) via a toggle button at the top. ================================================================================ 15. UNAUTHENTICATED) ================================================================================ The public homepage (https://creatora-frontend.vercel.app/) is composed of: 1. Hero Section — main headline/value proposition and primary sign-up call to action. 2. Features Section — six headline features presented to visitors: - "Learn Your Voice" — upload past content so Creatora learns unique writing style, tone, and personality. - "Generate On-Brand" — content that sounds authentically like the creator, not generic AI output. - "Weekly Planning" — plan a content calendar with AI-suggested topics and optimal posting times. - "Platform Optimized" — content tailored per platform (Twitter threads, LinkedIn posts, blog articles, etc.). - "Lightning Fast" — generate weeks of content in minutes; beat writer's block. - "Engagement Insights" — track what resonates with the audience and refine strategy. (Note: "Engagement Insights"/analytics as a dedicated dashboard is marketing copy on the landing page; a full analytics/insights feature was not found as a distinct implemented screen in the current dashboard — the closest real equivalent today is the Dashboard's stat cards and the AI-generated "posting schedule" reasoning.) 3. How It Works Section — a simplified step-by-step explainer of the onboarding → generation workflow described in Section 2/6 above. 4. Pricing Section — presents the product's pricing tiers/plans to visitors (mirrors the FREE/CREATOR/PRO plan concept). 5. CTA (Call To Action) Section — final sign-up prompt before the footer. 6. Footer and

→ generation workflow described in Section 2/6 above. 4. Pricing Section — presents the product's pricing tiers/plans to visitors (mirrors the FREE/CREATOR/PRO plan concept). 5. CTA (Call To Action) Section — final sign-up prompt before the footer. 6. Footer and Navbar are present site-wide. Auth-related public pages: - `/login` — email + password login form, with a link to `/forgot-password`. - `/signup` — account creation form (name, email, password). - `/forgot-password` — request an OTP reset email by entering an account email address. ================================================================================ 16. TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE (FOR REFERENCE / SUPPORT CONTEXT) ================================================================================ Backend: - Framework: NestJS 11 (Node.js, TypeScript), versioned REST API (`v1` prefix on most user-facing auth/profile/posts routes). - Database: PostgreSQL, accessed via Prisma ORM 6. - Authentication: JWT (via @nestjs/jwt + passport-jwt strategy), delivered as an HTTP-only cookie. - AI providers used: • Google Gemini (`@google/generative-ai` SDK) — models referenced in code: `gemini-2.5-flash` (content analysis, ideas, weekly planning) and `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` (posting schedule generation). • An external, separately hosted FastAPI microservice dedicated to generating the actual Instagram post assets (text/image/video variants), reachable at a Vercel-hosted URL referenced in the code as `creatora-fast-api-post-generation`. • An external, separately hosted "trending idea generation" GenAI microservice, reachable at a Render-hosted URL referenced in the code as `creatora-idea-genration-genai-model`. - Web scraping: Apify platform (`apify-client` SDK) with dedicated Actors per social platform (Instagram, Twitter/X

the code as `creatora-fast-api-post-generation`. • An external, separately hosted "trending idea generation" GenAI microservice, reachable at a Render-hosted URL referenced in the code as `creatora-idea-genration-genai-model`. - Web scraping: Apify platform (`apify-client` SDK) with dedicated Actors per social platform (Instagram, Twitter/X — with a fallback actor), plus custom scraping/cleanup logic for LinkedIn, YouTube, Facebook, and generic blog/website URLs (using `cheerio` for HTML parsing), with a final generic fallback scraper if the primary method errors. - Transactional email: Brevo (`@getbrevo/brevo` SDK) — used for password reset OTP emails and account deletion confirmation emails. - Image storage/CDN: Cloudinary. - API documentation: Swagger/OpenAPI, auto-generated and served at the `/docs` path of the backend (interactive "try it out" UI). - Deployment: Backend is deployed on Render (creatora-backend.onrender.com); configuration files in the repo also reference Railway and Nixpacks as alternative/additional deployment targets. Frontend: - Framework: Next.js 16 (App Router) with React 19. - Styling: Tailwind CSS v4, a custom design system with "glass card" UI components, gradient theming, and Radix UI primitives (Dialog, Slot, etc.) styled in a shadcn/ui-like pattern. - HTTP client: Axios, with `withCredentials: true` on requests so the HTTP-only auth cookie is sent automatically. - Notifications: `sonner` toast library for success/error feedback throughout the app. - Icons: `lucide-react`. - Deployment: Vercel (creatora-frontend.vercel.app). Data model summary (Prisma schema, PostgreSQL): - User — core account

true` on requests so the HTTP-only auth cookie is sent automatically. - Notifications: `sonner` toast library for success/error feedback throughout the app. - Icons: `lucide-react`. - Deployment: Vercel (creatora-frontend.vercel.app). Data model summary (Prisma schema, PostgreSQL): - User — core account (email, password hash, name, bio, credits, plan, profile image, active flag, password-reset OTP fields, timestamps). - BrandProfile — one-to-one with User; stores the AI-derived "voice" fingerprint described in Section 7. - SocialAccount — per-platform connected account metadata (platform, username, followers, engagement rate, post frequency, connection status, last synced time) — supports TWITTER, LINKEDIN, INSTAGRAM, FACEBOOK, YOUTUBE, BLOG, EMAIL, THREAD. (Note: this model exists in the schema to support future "connect your social accounts" functionality; no backend controller for connecting/syncing social accounts was found active in the current codebase — it appears to be a planned feature.) - PastPost — a past piece of content the user submitted during onboarding, including scraped content, platform, author, media URL, and the detailed Gemini analysis JSON. - ContentIdea — an AI or trending content idea (title, hook, description, format, angle, cta, platform, source, trend metadata, favorite flag). - GeneratedPost — a single AI-generated post record (its content stored as a JSON blob containing the text/image/video variants), linked optionally to the ContentIdea it was based on. - WeeklyPlan — one plan per user per week-start

metadata, favorite flag). - GeneratedPost — a single AI-generated post record (its content stored as a JSON blob containing the text/image/video variants), linked optionally to the ContentIdea it was based on. - WeeklyPlan — one plan per user per week-start date, storing the entire week's posts/reminders/unscheduled-idea-ids as a JSON blob. ================================================================================ 17. KNOWN LIMITATIONS / THINGS THE CHATBOT SHOULD BE CAREFUL ABOUT ================================================================================ - There is NO native, automatic publishing integration with Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Facebook, YouTube, or any other platform. "Posting" from Creatora is always a manual, user-driven action; the "Post to Instagram" button is a convenience helper (download image + copy caption + open Instagram) rather than a true API-based auto-publish. - The Account & Billing page is demo/mock data only (see Section 13) — do not claim real payments, invoices, or plan upgrades happen there today. - "Connect your social accounts" (the `SocialAccount` data model) does not currently have an active, user-facing connection flow in the codebase reviewed — treat this as a schema that exists for a feature not yet fully built/exposed, rather than confirming it as a live feature. - Content generation (`/content-generation/generate`) currently always targets Instagram-style assets in the frontend Generate screen; while the Brand Profile and prompts are platform-agnostic in principle, the polished "Generate Post" single-click experience in the UI today is built

as a live feature. - Content generation (`/content-generation/generate`) currently always targets Instagram-style assets in the frontend Generate screen; while the Brand Profile and prompts are platform-agnostic in principle, the polished "Generate Post" single-click experience in the UI today is built specifically around an Instagram feed-post format (image + caption + hashtags) plus an associated video/reel script. - AI outputs (ideas, posts, schedules, plans) are generated by large language models and, like any AI system, can occasionally produce imperfect, generic, or inaccurate suggestions; the backend includes fallback logic for parsing failures precisely because AI JSON output is not 100% guaranteed to be well-formed. - Email verification on signup does not appear to be implemented; accounts are active immediately after signup. ================================================================================ END OF DOCUMENT ================================================================================
